description: Structured UART maps, BEC outputs, gotchas, and configuration references for all Orqa flight controller hardware. Authoritative source for Orqa Mode in Wingman AI.

boards.name: Orqa QuadCore H7

bec_outputs.note: VTX only — 5V-only devices will be permanently damaged

uarts.note: Debug/USB only — never assign RX, GPS, VTX, or any peripheral here

uarts.recommended_use: DJI O3/O4, Walksnail Avatar, HDZero MSP OSD
uarts.note: Physical JST-GH connector on top of board — standard DJI pinout

uarts.recommended_use: SmartAudio VTX control or ESC telemetry RX
uarts.note: TX-only pad — use for SmartAudio or as ESC telemetry input (wire ESC TELE to FC TX5)

uarts.recommended_use: Available — telemetry, companion computer, or additional peripheral

gotchas: UART1 is VCP/USB — assigning any peripheral here will silently fail
9V BEC is for VTX only — GPS modules, receivers, LED strips, and most accessories are 5V-only; 9V will destroy them
DJI connector on UART2 requires MSP enabled at exactly 115200 baud in Betaflight Ports tab
Dual ICM-42688-P IMUs — enable second IMU in Betaflight gyro config for redundancy; not automatic
H7 generates significant heat — ensure thermal pad between FC and stack frame plate
Betaflight target ORQA_QUADCORE_H7 — do not use a generic STM32H743 target; pinout will be wrong
DSHOT600 bidirectional fully supported on H7 — set motor poles correctly for RPM filter to work
USB-C on top face — disconnect USB before arming; USB connected is an arming prevention flag
betaflight_cli_quickstart: resource SERIAL_TX 4 <UART4 TX pin for ELRS>

boards.name: Orqa 3030 Lite F405

bec_outputs.note: All outputs are 5V — no 9V BEC on this board

gotchas: F405 MCU — DSHOT600 bidirectional unreliable; use DSHOT300 for RPM filter; limit to 3 RPM harmonics
No 9V BEC — VTX must accept 5V; using a 5.8GHz VTX requiring 9-12V will damage it
UART1 is VCP only — never assign peripherals here
Betaflight target is ORQA_3030LITE_F405 — generic F405 target will have wrong pinout
Pairs mechanically with Orqa 3030 70A ESC on 30.5mm standoffs — M3 hardware
Single IMU — no redundancy; gyro failure = immediate loss of stabilization

boards.name: Orqa WingCore H7
boards.connectors: All JST-GH Pixhawk standard — NO solder pads

bec_outputs.note: Powered from servo rail or external BEC

jst_gh_ports.recommended_use: Primary telemetry — SiK radio, companion computer MAVLink
Receiver input — SBUS, CRSF, or ELRS
jst_gh_ports.note: Use RC connector, not TELEM, for receiver

jst_gh_ports.recommended_use: Direct connection to Orqa FPV.Connect VTX module
USB-C console access and firmware flash
DroneCAN bus — GPS, ESC, or sensor nodes
Primary power input with voltage/current sensing

gotchas: All connections are JST-GH Pixhawk standard — requires correct JST-GH cable assemblies; no solder fallback
Fixed-wing and VTOL controller — not intended for multirotors; use QuadCore H7 for quads
Orthogonal dual IMU for vibration rejection in fixed-wing flight — verify IMU orientation in QGC/MP
160A current sensor — calibrate in GCS before flight; default calibration may be off
PX4 target: orqa_wingcore_h7 | ArduPilot target: OrqaWingCoreH7 — must select exact target in flasher
VTOL transitions require careful parameter tuning — use PX4 VTOL framework documentation
Designed for Orqa MRM2-10 platform — motor/servo output mapping may differ on other airframes
DroneCAN requires 120-ohm termination at both ends of bus — check topology before enabling

boards.name: Orqa DTK APB
boards.description: All-in-one flight controller + companion computer. STM32H7 runs PX4; NXP i.MX8M Plus runs Linux with 2.3 TOPS NPU for onboard AI inference. MAVLink bridge between FC and companion is internal.
boards.expansion: PCIe — for cellular modems, additional AI accelerators, custom payloads
gotchas: FC and companion are on the same board — a firmware flash of the FC does NOT affect the Linux companion and vice versa
MAVLink bridge between H7 and i.MX8M is internal — no external UART wiring needed for companion-to-FC communication
Linux companion boots in ~20-30s — PX4 on the H7 boots faster; arming before Linux is fully up means no AI features
NPU (2.3 TOPS) runs NXP eIQ framework — models must be converted to NPU-compatible format (TFLite/ONNX via NXP tools)
PCIe expansion port allows adding cellular modem or additional compute — requires custom carrier design
Power architecture is critical — companion and FC share power rail; undervoltage affects both
Requires PX4 build configured for orqa_dtk_apb target — standard Pixhawk builds will not work
AI inference on NPU does not block FC control loop — runs asynchronously via MAVLink commands
use_cases: BVLOS with cellular connectivity via PCIe modem
AI Wingman edge deployment reference platform

boards.name: Orqa 3030 70A 4-in-1 ESC
gotchas: BLHeli_32 — bidirectional DSHOT supported from v32.7+; verify firmware version before enabling RPM filter
8-pin stack connector includes current sensor signal — connect CURR pin to FC CURR pad for battery current in OSD
70A continuous per motor — total 4-motor draw can reach 280A peak; ensure XT60 and wiring can handle this
NDAA compliant (Croatian manufacture) — verified for DOD procurement pathway alongside QuadCore H7
Motor direction set via BLHeli32 Suite or Betaflight motor direction override — not automatic on first flash
Temperature sensor outputs to telemetry — monitor ESC temp in Betaflight OSD to catch thermal issues
Pairs with Orqa QuadCore H7 and 3030 Lite F405 — 30.5mm standoffs, M3 hardware
